name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 78. **B7 INDIAN IT-PACK STRENGTH IS THE CYCLE-INTERNAL ROTATION SIGNAL — Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79%** among the top gainers when Sensex closed May 15 -0.21% / Nifty -0.19%. **Interpretation**: US recession risk priced through Indian IT services (rupee-revenue tailwind from USD/INR ₹95.96 + flight-to-defensive within Indian equity bucket). This is the FIRST cycle session where Indian IT outperformed in a global stagflation-shock session — possibly the first sign of the AI-cycle-decoupling-from-India headwind beginning to invert, but more likely a one-session rupee-revenue defensive bid. **Nifty Pharma defensive bid intact; Nifty IT remains -3% YTD vs Nifty -14% YTD = the cycle-bear case on Indian IT is structural (US AI-pricing-pressure + AI-cycle bypassing Indian services), but the May 15 session is an internal-rotation signal worth tracking.** SMH $556.34 -3.8% 1D — Indian IT diverged from US tech selloff on the day, which is unusual and supports the rupee-revenue-defensive read. NVDA earnings May 20 next major catalyst. PRIOR (May 7): **AI-CYCLE DECOUPLING FROM INDIA STRUCTURALLY HARDENS — KOSPI +37.3% / TAIEX +26.2% / SMH +37.5% (1M) WHILE NIFTY UNDERPERFORMS RISK-ON.** Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks (Bloomberg May 5, CNBC corroborated) extend halo to Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack, NOT to Indian IT services. Samsung Q1 net profits 57.2T KRW (~$39B) — single quarter higher than ALL of 2025 combined; Samsung market cap crossed $1T May 6 on KOSPI 7,400 (+6.7%). Nikkei +5.7% Golden Week catch-up (SoftBank +13-16%, Advantest +8%, Tokyo Electron +9%) is international validation of AI-cycle decoupling. **For India deployment thesis:** AI flows continue to bypass India IT services and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech — Indian-equity allocation continues to underperform structurally in this regime. Patel/SemiAnalysis: May 4-6 sequence is structural confirmation; Apple-Samsung-Intel talks specifically signal a US-AI-manufacturing-onshore tailwind that will rotate flows further from Indian IT services. Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro, HCL Tech) remains dominant DOMESTIC driver. The peace-dividend session today did NOT lift Indian IT — direct-vs-indirect bifurcation persists.
